item.title: Iran War Lifts Key Japanese Aluminum Premium to 11-Year High
item.description: Japan’s aluminum buyers agreed to pay the highest premium in 11 years after the Iran war disrupted supplies, a cost that’s likely to stoke inflationary pressures at the factories which use the metal.

item.title: Two Trades Likely Drove Biggest Indonesia Outflow in 21 Years
item.description: Indonesian stocks recorded their biggest foreign outflow in more than two decades, likely driven by block trades in palm oil maker PT FAP Agri, amid heightened scrutiny on ownership in the wake of MSCI Inc.’s investability concerns.

item.title: Singapore Looks to Host Central-Bank Gold Reserves to Boost Hub
item.description: Singapore is planning to expand its gold-storage capacity to become a custodian of bullion held by foreign central banks, part of a broader push by the city-state to compete with Hong Kong as a regional hub for the precious metal.

item.title: India Cuts Fuel Taxes to Shield Refiners From Iran War Impact
item.description: India has cut taxes on diesel and gasoline to cushion the impact of surging crude prices on its refiners, as a deepening conflict in the Middle East disrupts global supply.

item.title: Copper Heads for First Weekly Gain Since Start of Iran War
item.description: Copper rose on Friday — and was headed for its first weekly gain this month — on optimism that US efforts to end the war in Middle East would bear fruit and prevent a slowdown in global growth.

item.title: Treasurer Says Philippine Central Bank Hold May Calm Bond Market
item.description: The Philippine central bank’s decision to hold off raising interest rates on Thursday may help stabilize the government bond market after yields surged since the start of the Iran war, the country’s national treasurer said.

item.title: China’s March LNG Imports Poised for 8-Year Low as Prices Spike
item.description: China’s liquefied natural gas imports this month are on pace to hit the lowest level since 2018, according to ship-tracking data compiled by Kpler, as a price spike caused by the conflict in the Middle East curbs demand.

item.title: Australia Ramps Up Private Credit Scrutiny, Demanding More Data
item.description: Australia’s corporate regulator is boosting supervision of the private credit industry by requesting more detailed data from funds on a weekly basis as scrutiny grows on the $1.8 trillion industry worldwide.

item.title: Japan to Allow More Coal-Fired Power to Cope With Energy Shock
item.description: Japan will allow more use of coal-fired power plants in an effort to boost security of supply to cope with the energy shock from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Rupee Emerges as Achilles Heel for India Stock Bulls
item.description: Recent currency gains likely to be temporary unless external pressure eases.

item.title: Innovent Shares Jump on First Profit, Obesity Drug Momentum
item.description: Innovent Biologics Inc. shares saw their biggest gains in nearly five months, after the Chinese drugmaker posted its first annual profit thanks to surging demand for its weight-loss drug.

item.title: China Solar Wrap: Polysilicon Prices Decline on Bearish Outlook
item.description: Chinese polysilicon prices fell for a fourth week on persistent bearish sentiment around the supply and demand outlook.

item.title: DBS Joins Buzzing India ECM Space With Mandate in $1 Billion IPO
item.description: DBS Group Holdings Ltd. has entered India’s buoyant equity capital market, marking a push into one of the world’s busiest venues for share sales.

item.title: Ping An of China Prefers Short-Term Debt to Dodge Iran War Risk
item.description: Ping An of China Asset Management (Hong Kong) plans to ramp up purchases of short-term debt issued by Chinese banks to shield its investments from market volatility sparked by the Iran war.

item.title: China’s Industrial Profits Jumped Before Iran War Disruption
item.description: China’s industrial enterprises saw their profits rise sharply in the first two months of 2026, before the war in the Middle East rocked the global oil market and sent raw material costs soaring.

item.title: Japan’s Katayama Flags Possible Intervention as Yen Nears 160
item.description: Japan’s finance minister flagged the possibility of taking “bold actions” to counter currency moves, a reference to intervention, as the yen traded near a threshold where authorities entered the market on several occasions in 2024.

item.title: Pop Mart Launches Largest Ever Buyback After Record Stock Plunge
item.description: Pop Mart International Group Ltd. undertook its largest buyback ever in a move to bolster confidence after a record stock plunge spurred by concerns over its reliance on Labubu dolls for growth.

item.title: China Vanke, Country Garden Navigate Persistent Property Headwinds
item.description: Persistent weakness in China’s housing market will be the main drag when property developers China Vanke Co., Country Garden Holdings Co. and China Overseas Land & Investment Ltd. report next week.

item.title: Taiwan’s Largest ETF Set for Record Inflow, Defying War Angst
item.description: Taiwan’s largest equity exchange-traded fund is on track for record inflows this month, as domestic investors defy the global turmoil sparked by the Iran war and pile into the technology-heavy product.

item.title: Australia’s Fuel Supply Secure in Near Term, Albanese Says
item.description: Australia has a secure supply of fuel over the near term, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said Friday, as he sought to reassure skittish households and businesses at a time of mounting reports of gasoline shortages.

item.title: Malaysia Says Iran Has Allowed Some of Its Ships Through Hormuz
item.description: Iran has allowed Malaysian vessels trapped in the Persian Gulf to return home through the Strait of Hormuz, the Southeast Asian nation’s prime minister said in a televised address on Thursday evening.

item.title: TDR, I Squared Are Said to Select Banks for Aggreko’s US IPO
item.description: TDR Capital and I Squared Capital have picked banks to work on a US initial public offering for Aggreko Plc, one of the world’s biggest power generator rental firms, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Fund That Bet Big on Samsung Pushes for ADRs to Raise Valuation
item.description: A fund manager that has made Samsung Electronics Co. its biggest bet wants the South Korean company to list its shares in the US, a move it says would raise the chipmaker’s valuation.

item.title: Claude AI Maker Anthropic Said to Weigh IPO as Soon as October
item.description: Anthropic PBC is considering going public as soon as in October, according to people familiar with the matter, as the artificial intelligence company races with rival OpenAI Inc. to hold an initial public offering.

item.title: China Commerce Minister Protests US Probes Ahead of Trump Visit
item.description: Chinese Commerce Minister Wang Wentao raised “serious concerns” with US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer over new trade investigations, even as both sides signal a desire to stabilize relations before an expected presidential summit.

item.title: China’s Carmakers Return to Growth in Europe With EV Sales
item.description: Chinese automakers regained momentum in Europe in February, increasing pressure on Western incumbents after a brief sales slowdown at the start of the year.

item.title: MSCI Deadline Tests Indonesia’s Resolve to Fix Opaque Ownership
item.description: A looming MSCI Inc. decision over Indonesia’s market status is highlighting the challenge regulators face to address opaque ownership structures and poor disclosures by the nation’s biggest companies.

item.title: China Edges Toward Reflation, Boosting Stocks and Profit Outlook
item.description: China’s long-awaited shift toward reflation may finally be near, raising hopes it can revive corporate earnings and spur gains in stocks.

item.title: Dollar’s Best Month Since July Roils Wall Street’s FX Roadmap
item.description: The dollar is on track for its best month since July as the conflict in the Middle East scrambles Wall Street’s playbook for the world’s dominant reserve currency.

item.title: Pernod, Jack Daniel’s Maker Brown-Forman Are in Merger Talks
item.description: Pernod Ricard SA and Brown-Forman Corp., the owner of Jack Daniel’s whiskey, are discussing a merger as the alcoholic drink companies look at ways to consolidate amid an industry downturn.
